$comment: SINGLE SOURCE for the Xplor Hub — Login flow handoff. Edit this only; scripts/build-handoff.mjs generates HANDOFF.md, handoff.html, handover/*.json. Bump version + add a changelog row on every change.
title: Xplor Hub — Login flow
summary: Landscape tablet kiosk (1103×768) login flow for the Xplor Hub at a childcare service. A shared Dashboard offers QR sign-in, email sign-in and a visitor log; one email login branches to parent/contact attendance (role select → child sign-in) or the educator shift screen, while the visitor journey runs without login. 'Done' = all three journeys are walkable end to end, including the no-shift empty state and the sign-in Required error. One approved direction: light theme.
outOfScope: Real authentication and account/role lookup (prototype uses sanitized demo credentials only)
QR sign-in with Xplor Home (the QR tile is a decorative placeholder)
Shift/roster data behind End Shift / Resume Shift (static demo times)
Visitor record persistence (the visitor list resets when a journey relaunches)

components.name: hub-shell
components.notes: Kiosk screen chrome shared by every screen: 4px teal top strip, eyebrow (service label) or back-link, 48px title, top-right account avatar with presence dot; body scrolls, optional vertical centring for sparse screens.

components.name: hub-panel
components.notes: White bordered panel container (radius 20, border-default) hosting each screen's card content.

components.name: hub-action-card
components.notes: Large tappable action card: 64px icon tile + title/sub + trailing chevron. Used for Sign in with email, Visitor log, New visitor, End Shift (danger), Resume Shift (solid teal).

components.name: hub-person-card
components.notes: Role/child selection card: 64px photo (initials fallback), name, sub, status pill, trailing chevron or text action (Sign In / Casual Session). Rendered as a div with role=button so the trailing link can nest legally.

components.name: hub-visitor-row
components.notes: Visitor list row: 48px initials tile, name, 'Signed in {time}', trailing Sign out text link.

components.name: hub-field
components.notes: Outlined input with a notched floating label (matches the Figma InputTextSelect, Material-style). Differs from the shipped ds-input (outside label) — reconcile before build.

components.name: ds-pill
components.notes: Session/status tag (Booked Session / Casual Session) mirrors ds-pill minimal at 24px in cyan and orange tones — reuse ds-pill rather than the prototype's inline pill.

components.name: ds-btn
components.notes: Primary Login / Sign in button is a full-width 48px pill with a loading spinner + 'Signing in…' label — map to ds-btn--solid with a full-width variant.

motion.notes: Launch splash (XPLOR wordmark + spinner + 'Getting things ready…') replays on every journey launch: fade-out starts at 550ms, done at 950ms; skipped in ?bare=1 and on deep-links to non-dashboard steps. Each screen rises in over 400ms. Toasts auto-dismiss after 1.6s. Login shows a 1s loading state. prefers-reduced-motion disables all of it.

screens.name: Dashboard
screens.purpose: Kiosk landing screen: QR sign-in with Xplor Home, email sign-in, or the visitor log.
screens.entry: Every journey starts here (all three bottom launchers land on the Dashboard, behind the launch splash)
screens.exit: → S2 via Sign in with email; → S7 via Visitor log
layout: Hub shell: 4px teal strip · eyebrow 'Little Bugs ELC' · 48px title · account avatar (top right)
Centred panel (max 800px): QR tile (196px, decorative placeholder) + 'Scan with Xplor Home' caption on the left
Right column: two stacked action cards — Sign in with email · Visitor log

copy.text: Sign your name in or out

behaviour: The launch splash overlays this screen for ~950ms on every journey launch, then fades out.
'Sign in with email' opens the sign-in screen (S2) within the current journey (parent/contact or educator).
'Visitor log' switches to the visitor journey and opens the log (S7) — no login required.
The QR tile is decorative in the prototype; real pairing with Xplor Home is out of scope.
a11y: Action cards are real <button type="button"> elements.
The QR image is decorative — mark aria-hidden in build.
Kiosk touch targets: both action cards exceed 48px height.

acceptance.text: Sign in with email opens the sign-in screen.
acceptance.assert: click('Sign in with email') -> step == signin

acceptance.text: Visitor log opens the visitor journey without a login step.
acceptance.assert: click('Visitor log') -> step == visitor AND no credential prompt

acceptance.text: The splash replays on every journey launch and dismisses itself.
acceptance.assert: launch(journey) -> splash visible, auto-dismissed within ~1s; absent with ?bare=1

screens.name: Sign in
screens.purpose: Email + password login for parents, contacts and educators.
screens.entry: From the Dashboard (Sign in with email)
screens.exit: → S3 (parent/contact journey) or S5 (educator journey) after a 1s loading state; back → S1
layout: Hub shell with back-link 'Dashboard'; title 'Good Morning'; body vertically centred

464px login card: XPLOR wordmark · 'Little Bugs Login' · Forgot email? link · Email field · Forgot password? link · Password field (show/hide eye) · inline error slot · full-width Login button · helper paragraph

copy.text: Your centre username is also used to login to Playground and can be found under Service Settings on Xplor Office.

redlines.spec: 56px height, 1.5px border (grey-500 → action-primary on focus, orange-900 when invalid), notched floating label

behaviour: Both fields are pre-filled with the sanitized demo credentials (jessica.c@example.com / demo-1234).
Login with either field empty shows the inline 'Required' error and paints the empty field's border + label in the danger colour; no navigation.
Valid login shows the button loading state (spinner + 'Signing in…') for 1s, then routes to Role select (S3) — or to the Educator screen (S5) when the educator journey is active.
The eye button toggles the password field between masked and plain text.
The back link returns to the Dashboard and clears the error.
a11y: The eye toggle needs an aria-label ('Show password' / 'Hide password') and pressed state.
The inline 'Required' error must be associated with the invalid field (aria-describedby) and announced (role=alert).
Error state must not rely on the red border alone — the error text stays.
Never persist or log real credentials on a shared kiosk; demo values are placeholders only.

acceptance.text: Empty email or password blocks login with the Required error.
acceptance.assert: clear(field); click(Login) -> inline text 'Required' visible AND invalid field border == danger AND step == signin

acceptance.text: Valid login shows a ~1s loading state then routes by journey.
acceptance.assert: click(Login) -> button shows spinner + 'Signing in…'; after ~1s step == role (parent/contact) OR educator (educator journey)

acceptance.text: The eye button toggles password visibility.

screens.name: Role select
screens.purpose: After login, choose who is signing children in: parent or collection contact.
screens.entry: From a successful login on the parent/contact journey
screens.exit: → S4 via either role card; back → S1
layout: Hub shell with back-link 'Dashboard'; title 'Attendance'
Section label 'Role select'
Two-column grid of person cards: Parent (Jessica Jones · James May) · Collection Contact (Samuel Weinhandl), each with a Casual Session pill and trailing chevron

redlines.spec: 64px, radius 12, initials fallback (no external avatar service)

behaviour: Tapping either role card opens Child select (S4).
Photos fall back to a cyan initials tile — the prototype loads no external avatar assets.
a11y: Person cards are divs with role=button + tabIndex — build must add Enter/Space key activation (prototype gap).
Each card's accessible name should combine role + people (e.g. 'Parent — Jessica Jones and James May').

acceptance.text: Both role cards open Child select.
acceptance.assert: click(card Parent) -> step == child; click(card Collection Contact) -> step == child

screens.name: Child select
screens.purpose: Sign children in — booked sessions plus casual sign-in for children without a booking.
screens.exit: Stays on screen (toast per sign-in); back → S3
layout: Hub shell with back-link 'Dashboard' (returns to Role select); title 'Attendance'
Section 'Bookings today': 2-col grid of child cards (photo, name, session time, 'Booked Session' cyan pill, trailing 'Sign In' link)
Section 'No bookings': child cards with an orange 'Casual Session' pill and a trailing 'Casual Session' link

redlines.spec: 24px; cyan tone for booked, orange tone for casual

behaviour: 'Sign In' on a booked child shows the toast '{name} signed in' and stays on this screen (the action stops propagation — the card itself does not navigate).
Children without a booking carry the orange Casual Session pill and sign in via the 'Casual Session' action, with the same toast.
The back link returns to Role select (S3) despite its 'Dashboard' label — see open questions.
a11y: The toast is visual only — build needs aria-live=polite so sign-ins are announced.
Sign In / Casual Session are text buttons nested in a role=button card — keep them independently focusable with distinct names (e.g. 'Sign in Jessica Jones').

acceptance.text: Booked and no-booking children are visually distinct.
acceptance.assert: booked rows have cyan 'Booked Session' pill + 'Sign In' action; no-booking rows have orange 'Casual Session' pill + 'Casual Session' action

acceptance.text: Signing a child in confirms with a toast and stays on the screen.
acceptance.assert: click('Sign In') -> toast '{name} signed in' visible, auto-dismissed ~1.6s, step == child

screens.name: Educator — start shift
screens.purpose: Educator lands here after login to manage their shift: end it or resume it.
screens.entry: From a successful login on the educator journey
screens.exit: → S1 via End Shift or Resume Shift; back → S1
layout: Hub shell with back-link 'Dashboard'; title 'Welcome Educator'; body vertically centred
Panel (max 810px): date line · large clock · shift progress bar (6:00am → 6:00pm, ~40% elapsed)
Two action cards side by side: End Shift (danger tile) · Resume Shift (solid teal tile)

redlines.spec: 11px track (grey-300), teal fill to 40%, 15px white knob with 3px teal ring

behaviour: End Shift and Resume Shift both return to the Dashboard in the prototype — real confirmation/rota behaviour is an open question.
Date, clock and shift progress are static demo values.
a11y: The shift progress bar needs role=progressbar with aria-valuenow/min/max in build.
End Shift is a destructive action — confirm whether it needs a confirmation step on a shared kiosk.

acceptance.text: Both shift actions return to the Dashboard.
acceptance.assert: click('End Shift') -> step == dashboard; click('Resume Shift') -> step == dashboard

screens.name: Educator — no shift (empty state)
screens.purpose: Empty state when the logged-in educator has no shift configured in rostering.
screens.entry: Shown instead of S5 when the roster has no shift for the educator (prototype: reachable via the jump rail only — see open questions)
layout: Hub shell with back-link 'Dashboard'; title 'Welcome Educator'; body vertically centred
464px centred panel: heading · inline SVG illustration (roster card) · helper paragraph; no primary action

copy.text: To start a shift, make sure your centre administrator has one configured in the rostering.

behaviour: Terminal empty state — the only exit is the back link to the Dashboard.
In the prototype this screen is reached via the left jump rail; the real Hub must branch here from login when the roster lookup returns no shift.
a11y: The helper text carries the recovery instruction; keep it programmatically associated with the heading.

acceptance.text: The empty state offers no shift actions.
acceptance.assert: screen shows 'No shifts to begin' + helper text AND contains no End/Resume Shift controls

screens.name: Visitor log
screens.purpose: Log of today's visitors: sign a new visitor in, or sign an existing one out.
screens.entry: From the Dashboard (Visitor log) — no login required
screens.exit: → S8 via the Sign in card; back → S1
layout: Hub shell with back-link 'Dashboard'; title 'Visitor Log'
Full-width action card: Sign in — New visitor (solid teal tile)
Section label 'Afternoon' over a stacked list of visitor rows (initials tile, name, 'Signed in {time}', Sign out link)

redlines.spec: 16px between rows; 28px below the Sign in card

behaviour: Sign out removes the visitor's row immediately and shows the toast '{name} signed out'.
The Sign in card opens the New Visitor form (S8).
Signing out every visitor leaves the 'Afternoon' section empty — no designed empty state (see open questions). The list resets when a journey relaunches.
a11y: Each Sign out needs a distinct accessible name ('Sign out William Walker').
Row removal + toast must be announced (aria-live=polite).

acceptance.text: Sign out removes the row and confirms with a toast.
acceptance.assert: click('Sign out' on row) -> row removed AND toast '{name} signed out' visible

acceptance.text: The Sign in card opens the New Visitor form.

screens.name: New visitor
screens.purpose: Capture a new visitor's details and sign them in.
screens.entry: From the Visitor log (Sign in — New visitor)
screens.exit: → S7 via Sign in; back → S7
layout: Hub shell with back-link 'Dashboard' (returns to the Visitor log); title 'New Visitor'
Full-width panel: First Name + Last Name side by side · Phone Number · Address · Reason for Visit (textarea) · full-width Sign in button
behaviour: Sign in returns to the Visitor log (S7). The prototype performs no field validation and does not append the visitor to the list — see open questions.
Form values reset when a journey relaunches.
The 'Where you live?' / 'Why you here?' placeholders are carried verbatim from the Figma — flag for a copy review before build.
a11y: Each field's floating label is its programmatic label — keep the <label> association.
Visitor details are personal information on a shared kiosk — do not echo them back on screen after submission, and confirm the retention policy with engineering before build.

acceptance.text: Sign in returns to the Visitor log.

acceptance.assert: fill(fields); relaunch(journey); open S8 -> all fields empty

transitions.on: educator: valid login, no rostered shift

stateMatrix.trigger: Login with an empty email or password
stateMatrix.ui: inline error under the fields + danger field border/label

stateMatrix.trigger: Educator logs in with no shift configured in rostering
stateMatrix.ui: centred panel: heading + illustration + helper, no primary action
stateMatrix.copy: No shifts to begin — To start a shift, make sure your centre administrator has one configured in the rostering.
stateMatrix.recovery: Back to Dashboard; administrator configures a shift

stateMatrix.ui: section label with an empty list (undesigned)

platformDeltas.web: Fixed landscape tablet kiosk, 1103×768 logical px; prototype and this handoff are web-only
platformDeltas.ios: n/a — not specced in this handoff
platformDeltas.android: n/a — not specced in this handoff

openQuestions.q: S6 (no shift) has no in-flow trigger in the prototype (jump-rail only) — the real Hub must branch S2 → S6 on the roster lookup after educator login. Confirm the branching rule.
The prototype routes login by the active journey (parent vs educator launcher). The real Hub has one login — role must come from the account. Confirm role detection.
S4/S8 back-links are labelled 'Dashboard' but return one step (Role select / Visitor log). Confirm label vs target.
New visitor form ships with no validation and doesn't append to the visitor list — define required fields and the post-submit state.
Visitor log has no designed empty state once everyone signs out — design or accept the bare section label.
End Shift is destructive on a shared kiosk with no confirmation — confirm whether it needs one.
hub-field uses a notched floating label (per the Figma) while the shipped ds-input uses an outside label — reconcile before build.

decisions.decision: Demo credentials are sanitized placeholders: jessica.c@example.com / demo-1234 (example.com is RFC-2606 reserved).
decisions.rationale: This is a public repo — no real accounts, credentials or PII may appear in the prototype or handoff.

decisions.decision: No external avatar/photo service — person photos render as cyan initials tiles.
decisions.rationale: No network assets beyond what the repo already loads; kiosks may be offline-flaky.

decisions.decision: One visual direction (light theme, v=0) — no direction switcher in the harness.
decisions.rationale: The Hub kiosk ships a single treatment; the harness spends its chrome on a jump rail + journey launchers instead.

decisions.decision: All three journeys start from the shared Dashboard rather than separate entry screens.
decisions.rationale: Matches the kiosk reality: one idle screen, three intents branching off it.

changelog.change: Initial handoff: 8 screens across three journeys (parent/contact attendance, educator shift, visitor log), incl. the no-shift empty state and the sign-in Required error.
